# Handoff: Wax Mixer — animated DJ-setup graphic (Music Spot)

## Overview
A decorative, looping **DJ-setup illustration** — two turntables flanking a center mixer, with
the two vinyl records spinning continuously. The vinyls read **MUSIC / SPOT**; both turntable
bodies are labelled **M•S MUSIC**. Intended use: a website **loading / hero decoration**.

It is a faithful, fully self-contained recreation of a hand-drawn reference GIF, rebuilt as
**vector (SVG) generated by JS** so it stays crisp at any size and animates smoothly (the original
was a raster GIF).

## About the Design Files
The bundled file `Wax Mixer.html` is a **design reference / working prototype** — a single
self-contained HTML page (no build step, no external fonts, no images, no network requests). The
task is to **recreate it as a component in the Music Spot codebase** (`jaejun44/musicspot-app`,
Next.js 14 + TypeScript + Tailwind + Framer Motion), using that project's patterns — *not* to ship
this HTML file as-is. Because the artwork is pure inline SVG + CSS, porting is mostly a copy of the
generation logic into a React component that returns the SVG.

## Fidelity
**High-fidelity.** Final geometry, colors, stroke weights, and motion. Recreate pixel-faithfully.

## What's on screen
A single full-width graphic on a `viewBox="0 0 1920 737"` SVG, horizontally centered, `max-width:1680px`.
Three elements left→right:

1. **Left turntable** — light-gray body, black vinyl with concentric grooves, white center label,
   tonearm (top-right), pitch fader + buttons (right edge), 33/45 speed block (bottom-left),
   `M•S MUSIC` wordmark + ruler ticks (bottom-right). Vinyl lettering: **MUSIC** (upper arc) /
   **SPOT** (lower arc).
2. **Center mixer** — dark gray slab (`#616661`), 3×4 grid of knobs, two channel faders with
   red/green VU meter dashes, a bottom crossfader, RCA/headphone cables arcing off the top.
3. **Right turntable** — identical to the left, but its spinning label is **180° out of phase**
   (it appears upside-down relative to the left at any given moment).

### Hand-drawn look (important)
Every shape is drawn with a **seeded rough-path generator** (`mulberry32` seed → small per-vertex
jitter) so circles/rects/lines look hand-sketched. Left deck uses seed `11`, right deck seed `23`,
mixer seed `41` — keep these so the two decks stay visually distinct. The seed → identical output
every render, so it's deterministic.

### Lettering (no webfont)
The marker-style letters are **drawn as vector strokes**, not text — there is a tiny stroke-font in
the `GLY` object (only the glyphs `M U S I C P O T` + space + `•` exist). `drawWord(word, centerX,
baselineY, scale, bow, tilt, stroke, sw)` lays them on an arc. **Do not** swap these for a real
`<text>`/webfont unless you also accept a different look — the hand-drawn strokes are the design.
Adding new letters means adding entries to `GLY`.

> **Mobile note (the key fix):** stroke widths are authored in **viewBox units** (via
> `stroke-width = sw / scale`, *no* `vector-effect="non-scaling-stroke"`) so strokes scale *with*
> the letters. An earlier version used `non-scaling-stroke`, which kept strokes a fixed screen-px
> width while letters shrank on mobile — causing the text to mush together. Keep strokes scaling
> proportionally.

## Motion / behavior
- Only the **lettering group** (`.spin`) rotates; the black disc + grooves + white label are
  radially symmetric, so they look static while the words turn. This keeps the spin cheap.
- CSS keyframe `msspin` → `rotate(360deg)`, `animation: msspin var(--spin) linear infinite`.
- Rotation pivot: `transform-box: fill-box; transform-origin: center` on `.spin`. (An invisible
  symmetric `<circle r="170">` is included inside `.spin` so its fill-box center lands exactly on
  the platter center.)
- Speed: `--spin` CSS variable, default **0.8s per revolution** (≈75 rpm).
- Right deck offset: `animation-delay: calc(var(--spin) / -2)` → 180° phase.
- `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` → animation disabled (static). Honor this.

### Tweakable variants (currently wired as a dev "Tweaks" panel — port as component props)
- **Spin speed** — `--spin` (0.3s–2.4s).
- **Motion** — `smooth` (linear) vs `steppy` (`steps(8)` timing → choppy GIF-style).
- **Direction** — `forward` vs `reverse` (`animation-direction`).
- **Background theme** — `white` (original, default) vs `ink` (dark Music Spot theme; swaps
  body/ink colors + adds a faint grain overlay). See `THEMES` object.

> The in-page Tweaks panel + its `postMessage` host wiring are **prototype-only** — drop them when
> porting and expose `spinSpeed` / `motion` / `direction` / `theme` as component props instead.

## Design tokens (as built)
| Role | Value |
|---|---|
| Page background (default / `white`) | `#ffffff` |
| Turntable body fill | `#e6e4df` |
| Turntable body stroke / ink | `#232323` / `#1a1a1a` |
| Vinyl disc | `#050505`, grooves `#171717` |
| Vinyl center label | `#faf8f2` (stroke `#d8d4c8`) |
| Vinyl lettering | `#f4f1e9` (bone) |
| Strobe speckle dots | `#f2f0ea` |
| Mixer body | `#616661` (stroke `#3a3d3a`) |
| Mixer knobs | `#0b0b0b`, indicator `#f0f0f0` |
| VU meter | green `#3a9b46`, red `#c0271e` |
| Cables | RCA blue `#3aa6dd`, plug red `#c0271e`, cord `#111` |
| Dark theme (`ink`) bg / body / ink | `#0B0B0E` / `#17171c` / `#f4f1e9` |
| Spin period | `0.8s` (≈75 rpm) |
| Vinyl letter stroke `sw` | `6.5` (viewBox units) |
| Deck wordmark stroke `sw` | `3.6` |

> The dark `ink` theme intentionally matches the **Music Spot renewal design system** (near-black
> `#0B0B0E` canvas, warm-bone `#F4F1E9` ink). If you place this on a dark Music Spot page, use the
> `ink` theme.

## Assets
**None.** No images, icons, or fonts — everything is generated SVG. No external dependencies.

## Files
- `Wax Mixer.html` — the complete prototype. Key functions: `roughCircle / roughRect / roughLine`
  (hand-drawn primitives), `GLY` + `drawWord` (stroke lettering), `turntable(side, TH)`,
  `mixer(TH)`, `build()` (assembles the SVG), `render()` (applies tweak state). The `.spin` CSS
  block governs the animation.

## Suggested port (React, for reference)
Wrap `build()`'s output in a component that returns the SVG string via `dangerouslySetInnerHTML`,
or translate the generator to JSX. Expose `spinSpeed`, `motion`, `direction`, `theme` as props.
Keep the seeds (11/23/41) and the `sw/scale` stroke convention.
